name: Le Pic de Tarbesou - trail loop starting from the Col de Pailhères car park
name_original: Le Pic de Tarbesou – boucle trail au départ du parking du Col de Pailhères
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ascou are set within the French Pyrénées, offering a diverse landscape of mountains and valleys. The region features varied terrain, from gentle paths along rivers to more challenging ascents through natural areas. This environment provides numerous opportunities for running, with trails winding through different elevations. The area's natural features make it suitable for a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around Ascou The most popular jogging route is Le Pic de Tarbesou - trail loop starting from the Col de Pailhères car park, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through mountain scenery. Another top favourite among local runners is Lake Campauleil – Virgin's Rock loop from Orgeix, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This route features views of Lake Campauleil and surrounding natural landscapes. Local runners also love the Lake Campauleil – Orgeix loop from Orgeix, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through the Lake Campauleil area, often completed in about 40 minutes. Jogging around Ascou is defined by Pyrenean mountain scenery, diverse trails, and valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to difficult mountain runs. The routes in Ascou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Ascou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of running terrain can I expect in Ascou?
A: Ascou, nestled in the French Pyrénées, offers a diverse range of running terrain. You'll find everything from gentle paths along rivers to more challenging ascents through mountainous landscapes. The region is characterized by Pyrenean mountain scenery and varied trails, suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there many running routes available in Ascou?
A: Yes, Ascou boasts a comprehensive network of running routes. There are over 130 routes available, catering to various preferences and abilities. This includes 2 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 92 difficult routes for those seeking a challenge.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Ascou?
A: The running routes in Ascou are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2000 runners have explored Ascou's varied terrain using komoot, often praising the stunning mountain scenery and diverse trail options.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Ascou?
A: While many routes in Ascou offer a challenge, there are a few easier options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41536269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Campauleil – Orgeix loop from Orgeix&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.0-mile (6.4 km) trail with minimal elevation gain, making it a good choice for a more relaxed run.

Q: Can I find circular running paths in Ascou?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Ascou are designed as loops, offering convenient circular paths. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41537391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Campauleil – Virgin's Rock loop from Orgeix&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views and brings you back to your starting point.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Ascou?
A: Ascou's routes offer breathtaking Pyrenean mountain views. You can encounter various natural features and landmarks, including the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Campauleil&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pailhères Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also offer viewpoints over the Rabassoles ponds.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. Ascou is well-suited for experienced trail runners seeking a challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974169641/le-pic-de-tarbesou-trail-loop-starting-from-the-col-de-pailheres-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Pic de Tarbesou - trail loop starting from the Col de Pailhères car park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.3-mile (11.7 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering a strenuous and rewarding experience.

Q: What are some good options for moderate-difficulty running routes?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41537371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgeix – Camping des Ioules loop from Orgeix&lt;/a&gt;. This 10.6 km route provides a good workout without the extreme elevation of the most difficult trails, allowing you to enjoy the scenery at a comfortable pace.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of lakes?
A: Yes, several routes provide beautiful lake views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41537391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Campauleil – Virgin's Rock loop from Orgeix&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, featuring stunning vistas of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3822968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Campauleil&lt;/a&gt; and its surrounding natural landscapes.

Q: What kind of elevation changes should I expect on Ascou's running trails?
A: Elevation changes vary significantly across Ascou's trails. You can find relatively flat paths with minimal gain, such as the Lake Campauleil loops, or opt for routes with substantial ascents like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974169641/le-pic-de-tarbesou-trail-loop-starting-from-the-col-de-pailheres-car-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Pic de Tarbesou loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 600 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or high points I can reach on a run?
A: Yes, for those looking to conquer a peak, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15026796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarbésou Peak loop from Port de Pailhères&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging run to a high point. This difficult 6.7 km route includes nearly 400 meters of elevation gain, rewarding you with panoramic views.
tips: The Rabassoles ponds form a magnificent set of three ponds: the Rabassoles pond, the Blue pond and the Black pond.
The peak of Tarbésou, which culminates at 2,364 meters and overlooks the ponds of Rabassoles, offers a magnificent belvedere.
